items.title: USDA finalizes new SNAP retailer stocking standards; compliance required by Nov. 4, 2026
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service published a final rule updating SNAP retailer stocking standards, including requiring most SNAP-authorized retailers to carry at least seven varieties in each of four staple food categories and to offer perishable varieties in at least three categories. The rule is effective July 7, 2026, and retailers must implement the provisions no later than Nov. 4, 2026.

items.title: CMS issues guidance ahead of Oct. 1, 2026 limits on federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released guidance for states on implementing statutory limits on claiming federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or nationals or who fall into certain noncitizen categories. The change begins Oct. 1, 2026, and CMS notes exceptions including continued federal matching for treatment of emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to the option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people.

items.title: HUD changes documentation rules for Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher programs; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-05 removes the prior alternative policy that allowed admission without Social Security number or eligible immigration documentation and then provided up to 180 days after admission to supply documents. HUD directs public housing agencies to follow the standard SSN and citizenship/eligible immigration verification regulations, and requires compliance within 60 days of the notice’s March 5, 2026 publication.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: HUD publishes FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding provisions, including $34.557B for HAP renewals
items.summary: HUD published PIH Notice 2026-12 implementing FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) funding provisions, including $34,557 million for HAP renewal funding and $2,836 million for administrative fees. The notice also describes set-asides such as $601 million for Tenant Protection Vouchers and notes TPVs will only be provided in 2026 for vacant units that were occupied by an assisted family in the previous 12 months.

items.title: IRS opens 2026 tax filing season; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2 for many early filers
items.summary: The IRS opened the 2026 filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and began accepting and processing individual tax returns for tax year 2025. The IRS said most refunds for taxpayers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for many filers who choose direct deposit and have no issues with their returns, with Where’s My Refund? projected deposit dates available by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC cash-value benefit amounts; states must authorize at least one non-fresh fruit/veg form by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS issued WIC Policy Memorandum 2026-2 setting FY 2026 cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts, including $26 per month for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. The memo also notes state agencies must authorize at least one additional fruit and vegetable form (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SSA updates 2026 disability and SSI thresholds (SGA, trial work period, and SSI federal benefit rate)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 Red Book lists Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) at $1,690 per month for people with disabilities (non-blind) and $2,830 per month for people who are blind. The same page lists the trial work period monthly earnings amount as $1,210 and the SSI federal benefit rate at $994 per month for an eligible individual (and $1,491 for an eligible couple) for 2026.
